articles.title: Peri-implantitis-Is it mainly a clinician-initiated complication of implant therapy?
articles.summary: This review synthesizes evidence that many peri-implantitis cases result from clinician-controlled surgical and prosthetic errors—particularly exposure of micro-rough implant surfaces—rather than patient factors alone. The paper advocates specific evidence-based prevention protocols that implant practitioners can implement immediately to reduce this growing complication.

articles.title: Periodontitis, Edentulism, and Risk of Metabolic Syndrome, Obesity, and Dyslipidemia: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analyses.
articles.summary: Meta-analysis of longitudinal studies confirms that periodontitis and edentulism are associated with increased risk of metabolic syndrome, obesity, and dyslipidemia, strengthening the evidence for oral-systemic health relationships. This finding supports counseling patients about periodontal disease as a modifiable risk factor for metabolic diseases and cardiovascular health.

articles.title: Long-term outcomes of regenerative periodontal therapy and orthodontic treatment: 20-year results from a prospective study.
articles.summary: A 20-year prospective study demonstrated that combining periodontal regenerative surgery (enamel matrix derivative with or without bone grafting) followed by orthodontic correction of malposition yields durable clinical attachment and bone-level improvements in severely compromised teeth. This evidence supports an integrated regenerative-orthodontic approach for Stage IV periodontitis patients seeking to save pathologically mobile teeth.

articles.title: Clinical evidence of bone apposition and potential osseointegration in additively manufactured subperiosteal implants: a report of three cases.
articles.journal: International journal of oral and maxillofacial surgery
articles.summary: This case series presents the first direct clinical evidence that additively manufactured subperiosteal implants can achieve osseointegration in humans, challenging decades of historical assumptions about their biocompatibility. These custom implants may offer a viable alternative for severely resorbed ridges where endosteal implants are not feasible.

articles.title: Clinical and Psychosocial Predisposing Factors Associated With Anaesthesia Failure During Non-Surgical Root Canal Treatment: A National Dental Practice-Based Research Network Study.
articles.summary: A large practice-based network study (1,723 patients) identified patient demographic, psychosocial, and clinical predictors of local anesthesia failure during endodontic treatment, enabling dentists to anticipate and mitigate this common problem. These findings provide actionable guidance for improving clinical outcomes in routine endodontic practice.

articles.title: Regenerative periodontal surgery and orthodontic therapy in the treatment of patients with stage IV periodontitis and pathologic tooth migration.
articles.summary: This review provides guidance on the integrated, interdisciplinary management of advanced periodontitis with pathologic tooth migration, emphasizing the strategic sequencing of regenerative periodontal surgery and orthodontic therapy. Practicing clinicians can apply these principles to stage IV case type 2 patients, though treatment remains complex and requires specialist collaboration.

articles.title: Long-Term Associations between Water Fluoridation and Adult Dental Caries.
articles.summary: This study leverages the natural experiment of Ireland's different water fluoridation policies (Republic versus Northern Ireland) to examine long-term associations between community water fluoridation exposure and adult dental caries in populations with near-lifetime exposure. The findings provide robust population-level evidence regarding fluoridation's sustained protective effect on permanent tooth caries in adulthood.

articles.title: Association Between Change in Dental Hygiene Practices and Incidence of Dementia.
articles.summary: Adults who increased toothbrushing to 3 or more times daily showed significantly lower dementia risk over 14 years in a large Korean cohort study. This finding supports stronger public health messaging about oral hygiene and suggests a potential modifiable risk factor for cognitive decline.

articles.title: Surgical Reconstructive Peri-Implantitis Treatment for 3- and 4-Wall Bone Defects: A Single-Blind Randomised Controlled Trial With a 1-Year Follow-Up.
articles.summary: A randomized controlled trial found that reconstructive surgery with bone graft and membrane during peri-implantitis treatment improved bone level recovery and probing depth reduction at 1 year. This evidence supports the use of guided bone regeneration in surgical management of advanced peri-implant defects.

articles.title: Long-Term Outcomes of 6-mm Short Implants Supporting Splinted Crowns in Posterior Regions: A 12-Year Prospective Multi-Centre Single-Cohort Study.
articles.summary: A 12-year prospective multicenter study of 95 short (6 mm) implants supporting splinted crowns in posterior regions reported high implant survival and good patient satisfaction, demonstrating that short implants are a viable alternative to bone augmentation. These long-term clinical outcomes provide strong evidence for a technique that many general dentists and specialists can adopt for patients with limited vertical bone.
